name: Puentedey from Villarcayo — circular by the Monumento Natural Ojo Guareña — Las Merindades
name_original: Puentedey desde Villarcayo — circular por el Monumento Natural Ojo Guareña — Las Merindades
region: Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja traverse a diverse landscape situated between the Cantabrian Mountains and the plateau. The region is characterized by varied terrain, shaped by rivers such as the Ebro, Nela, and Trema, creating a harmonious and contrasting environment for cyclists. This area, part of the Las Merindades region, features a succession of mountains, valleys, and river gorges, offering a range of road cycling experiences. The routes often include paved sections and pass through natural features like the Nela River valley. Best road cycling routes around Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja The most popular road cycling route is Puentedey from Villarcayo — circular by the Monumento Natural Ojo Guareña — Las Merindades, a 41.0 miles (66.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a significant elevation gain of 2395 feet (730 meters). Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Quisicedo Church loop from Las Merindades, a moderate 38.5 miles (61.9 km) path. This route offers varied terrain with an elevation gain of 1572 feet (479 meters). Local road cyclists also love the Puentedey – Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey loop from Las Merindades, a 41.5 miles (66.7 km) trail leading through the Nela River valley, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Road cycling around Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja is defined by river valleys, natural rock formations, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes in the Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja area, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 6 easy routes, 32 moderate routes, and 17 more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in this region?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for road cycling, situated between the Cantabrian Mountains and the plateau. Routes traverse varied terrain shaped by rivers like the Ebro, Nela, and Trema, featuring harmonious and contrasting environments with river valleys, natural rock formations, and rolling hills. Most routes are on paved sections.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057534874/puentedey-from-villarcayo-circular-by-the-monumento-natural-ojo-guarena-las-merindades" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentedey from Villarcayo — circular by the Monumento Natural Ojo Guareña — Las Merindades&lt;/a&gt; is a 66 km loop with significant elevation gain, offering a comprehensive experience of the local landscape.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks I can see while road cycling here?
A: You can encounter several impressive natural landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey&lt;/a&gt;, where the Nela River carves through stone, is a highlight. The Nela River valley itself, with its natural park 'El Soto' in Villarcayo, offers scenic views. The spectacular Hocinos Gorge, formed by the Ebro River, is also nearby.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find routes that include waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20162985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Guarguero Waterfall – Villarcayo Riverside Beach loop from Las Merindades&lt;/a&gt; is an 88 km moderate route that will take you past the Pozo Guarguero Waterfall. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3970163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tartalés de los Montes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another natural attraction in the wider region.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Bernabé of Ojo Guareña&lt;/a&gt;, part of an extensive cave system. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of the Constables&lt;/a&gt; in Villarcayo, a 14th-century fortified palace, now houses the Historical Museum of the Merindades. Many surrounding villages also feature Romanesque churches and hermitages.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja?
A: The diverse landscape and generally pleasant climate make spring and autumn ideal for road cycling in Villarcayo. These seasons offer comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still providing good weather conditions for longer rides.

Q: Are there any easy, paved routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, there are 6 easy routes available, many of which are paved. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the presence of converted railway paths in the region, such as remnants of the former Santander-Mediterranean line, often provide flatter, scenic options suitable for less experienced cyclists or families.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja?
A: The road cycling routes in Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic river valleys, and the natural rock formations that make for engaging rides.

Q: Where can I find parking in Villarcayo if I'm bringing my bike?
A: Villarcayo, as the capital of Las Merindades, offers various parking options within the town center. While specific cyclist parking facilities are not detailed, general public parking areas are available, making it convenient to start your cycling adventures from the town.

Q: Can I reach Villarcayo De Merindad De Castilla La Vieja using public transport?
A: Villarcayo is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services connecting it to larger cities in the Burgos province. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes to Villarcayo, especially if you plan to bring your bike, as policies for transporting bicycles may vary.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: While many routes are moderate in length, the region offers options for longer rides by combining existing routes or exploring the extensive network. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20162985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Guarguero Waterfall – Villarcayo Riverside Beach loop from Las Merindades&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 88 km route, providing a good challenge for those seeking longer distances.
tips: The name of this small village of only 50 inhabitants means "Bridge of God". His most photographed image is the natural stone bridge created by the Nela River. On the arch the town was created. In 2022, the association The Most Beautiful Towns in Spain announced that Puentedey was added to its list.
In the Valle de Sotoscueva appears the town of Quisicedo. This is a small town located in the southern foothills of the Sierra Cantábrica.
